name: bioethics_cross_border_data_transfer_redwood_auto_ben
premise: A Bioethics ethics consult line is in a fast-moving incident involving bioethics cross border data transfer redwood auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases consent, harm minimization, and precedent. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Marco Santos
agents.identity: I am Marco Santos, accountable for a defensible call in Bioethics when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Mira Santos
agents.identity: I am Mira Santos, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Bioethics.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Marco Santos must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Marco Santos do?
